406	function handleKeyDown(event) {
407	  const tag = document.activeElement?.tagName;
408	  if (tag === "INPUT" || tag === "TEXTAREA" || tag === "SELECT") return;
409	
410	  // Export modal acts as a modal: Escape closes it, every other
411	  // shortcut is ignored while it's open.
412	  if (!exportModal.hidden) {
413	    if (event.key === "Escape") {
414	      event.preventDefault();
415	      closeExportModal();
416	    }
417	    return;
418	  }
419	
420	  // Privacy flip modal — Escape closes; all other shortcuts suppressed.
421	  // Typing inside the attribution textbox is already handled by the
422	  // INPUT short-circuit above.
423	  if (!privacyModal.hidden) {
424	    if (event.key === "Escape") {
425	      event.preventDefault();
426	      closePrivacyModal();
427	    }
428	    return;
429	  }
430	
431	  // New-bank modal — Escape closes; every other shortcut suppressed.
432	  if (!newBankModal.hidden) {
433	    if (event.key === "Escape") {
434	      event.preventDefault();
435	      closeNewBankModal();
436	    }
437	    return;
438	  }
439	
440	  // Delete-confirm acts as a modal: only Enter confirms and Escape cancels;
441	  // every other shortcut is ignored until the user resolves it.
442	  if (pendingDeleteTakeId) {
443	    if (event.key === "Enter") {
444	      event.preventDefault();
445	      doDelete(pendingDeleteTakeId);
446	    } else if (event.key === "Escape") {
447	      event.preventDefault();
448	      hideDeleteConfirm();
449	    }
450	    return;
451	  }
452	
453	  if (
454	    tag === "BUTTON" &&
455	    (event.key === "Enter" || event.key === " " || event.key === "Backspace")
456	  ) {
457	    // Let the focused button handle these keys natively.
458	    return;
459	  }
460	
461	  if (event.key === "ArrowUp" || event.key === "ArrowDown") {
462	    if (!currentBank?.config.phonemes?.length) return;
463	    event.preventDefault();
464	    const ids = currentBank.config.phonemes.map((p) => p.id);
465	    const idx = Math.max(0, ids.indexOf(selectedPhonemeId));
466	    const delta = event.key === "ArrowUp" ? -1 : 1;
467	    const next = (idx + delta + ids.length) % ids.length;
468	    selectPhoneme(ids[next]);
469	    return;
470	  }
471	
472	  if (event.key === "r" || event.key === "R") {
473	    if (!micStream) return;
474	    event.preventDefault();
475	    toggleRecording();
476	    return;
477	  }
478	
479	  if (event.key === " ") {
480	    if (!selectedTakeId) return;
481	    event.preventDefault();
482	    togglePlay(selectedTakeId);
483	    return;
484	  }
485

- M5 and M7 shipped in one commit — the drag handler is tightly coupled to the new `paintWaveformForSelectedTake`.
- Trim endpoint helpers adapted to this repo's idioms (`self._send_json(HTTPStatus, {...})`, `self.config.ffmpeg`, `self.config.repo_root`) rather than the plan's literal stubs.
- Trim bar styling uses the project's existing CSS vars (`--accent`, `--border`, `--good`) instead of the fallback values in the plan.

**What I couldn't do (needs you)**

_**User (2026-04-24 09:05:09Z)**_

Base directory for this skill: /Users/leocaseiro/Sites/ipa-phonemes-recorder/.claude/skills/handoff

# Handoff

Adapted from the [Cult of Claude handoff skill](https://cultofclaude.com/skills/handoff/) ([source](https://github.com/robertguss/claude-code-toolkit/blob/main/skills/handoff/SKILL.md)), tailored for BaseSkill's worktree + `/resync` + baby-step commit workflow.

## When to use

- Just saved a plan or spec (→ next session executes it)
- Just raised or merged a PR (→ next session starts follow-up work)
- Ending a work session or context-switching
- User says "hand this off", "continue in a new session", "pick this up later"

Explicit invocation: `/handoff` or `/continue`.

## Worktree rule

Handoffs are written inside the **active worktree**, never on `master`. If the current working directory is not under `worktrees/<name>/`, stop and ask the user whether to create a worktree first (see [CLAUDE.md](../../../CLAUDE.md) worktree gate).

## Process

### Step 1 — Auto-detect context

Run these commands and keep the output for the template:

```bash
git branch --show-current
git worktree list | grep "$(pwd)"
git status --porcelain | wc -l
git rev-list --count @{u}..HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo 0
git log -1 --pretty=format:"%h %s"
gh pr view --json number,state,url 2>/dev/null || true
```

Also scan `docs/superpowers/specs/` and `docs/superpowers/plans/` for recently-modified files whose slug matches the current branch or task (optional — include only if clearly relevant).

### Step 2 — Ask one question

> "Anything specific you want captured? (key decisions, gotchas, things you'll forget)"

Single prompt, no follow-up loop. The user can answer briefly or skip.

### Step 3 — Generate the document

Fill in this template. **Omit any section with no content** — keep the document scannable.

````markdown
# Handoff: <brief description>

**Date:** YYYY-MM-DD
**Branch:** <branch-name>
**Worktree:** worktrees/<name>
**Worktree path:** <absolute path>
**Git status:** clean | N uncommitted | N unpushed commits ahead of origin
**Last commit:** <sha> <subject>
**PR:** #NNN — open|merged|closed (omit if none)

## Resume command

```
cd worktrees/<name>
<next action — e.g., execute plan docs/…md, open PR, resume debugging>
```

## Current state

**Task:** <what we're working on>
**Phase:** exploration | planning | implementation | debugging | review | shipped
**Progress:** <milestone or %>

## What we did

<2–3 sentences>

## Decisions made

- **<decision>** — <why>

## Spec / Plan

- docs/superpowers/specs/YYYY-MM-DD-<topic>-design.md
- docs/superpowers/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-<topic>.md

## Key files

- src/…/foo.ts:42 — <what and why>

## Open questions / blockers

- [ ] <question>

## Next steps

1. [ ] <first action>
2. [ ] <second>

## Context to remember

<constraints, user preferences, domain knowledge not obvious from the code>
````

### Step 4 — Write the file

Save to `.claude/handoffs/YYYY-MM-DD-<brief-slug>.md`. Slug is 2–5 lowercase words joined by hyphens, matching the branch topic.

### Step 5 — Commit as a baby step

```bash
git add .claude/handoffs/YYYY-MM-DD-<slug>.md
git commit -m "docs(handoff): <brief description>"
```

### Step 6 — Print the resume hint

Tell the user:

> Saved handoff: `.claude/handoffs/YYYY-MM-DD-<slug>.md` — paste that path in a new session to resume.

## Quality check (before writing)

Mental pass — no validation script:

1. Could a fresh Claude (or Cursor) pick up from this?
2. Are decisions traceable (the "why", not just the "what")?
3. Are next steps actionable and specific?
4. Are file paths anchored (`foo.ts:42`, not "that function")?

If any answer is "no", tighten the relevant section before saving.

## What to capture

### Always

- Decisions with reasoning — the "why"
- File paths touched, with intent
- Current progress — where we stopped
- Actionable next steps
- User context — constraints, preferences, domain knowledge

### When relevant

- Errors hit and how they resolved (or didn't)
- Dead ends — approaches tried that didn't work (saves re-exploration)

### Skip

- Verbose tool output
- Intermediate reasoning that reached conclusions
- Information obvious from the code


---

Spec bug confirmed. Per [docs/specs/2026-04-24-take-trim-design.md:63](docs/specs/2026-04-24-take-trim-design.md#L63), `⏮`/`Home` should jump to **clip** start (timeline 0) and `⏭`/`End` to clip end (`durationMs`). The plan had them jump to selection edges, which conflicts with `,`/`.` (those *do* jump to trim start/end).

Fixing in [ui/main.js](ui/main.js).

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-04-24 09:13:14Z)**_

Pushed four fixes to [PR #4](https://github.com/leocaseiro/ipa-phonemes-recorder/pull/4):

1. **Jump ⏮/⏭ now match the spec** — playhead jumps to clip 0 / clip duration (not selection edges).
2. **`repaintTrim` no longer re-decodes the WAV** — uses the cached `selectedTakeBuffer` and paints synchronously. Removes a race where the overlay lagged behind state changes, which was probably what "not working" meant.
3. **Playhead can roam the whole clip** — nudge (arrow keys) and canvas drag no longer clamp it to `[startMs, endMs]`, so "jump to 0 → nudge right → set `[`" now works.
4. **Playhead is now a visible red 2px line** — was a 1px white line that got occluded by the orange handles when they overlapped at the edges (very likely why you thought "nothing happened").

Refresh the UI and try again: click anywhere on the waveform to move the red playhead, then `[` / `]` (or the buttons) should snap the orange handles to it.

---

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-04-24 09:26:06Z)**_

Found the mouse bug. In `hitTest`, the tolerance treats a click up to 40ms **past** the end handle as a handle grab — so clicking just past the end actually drags the handle, not the playhead. I'll tighten `hitTest` so handles can only be grabbed when you click *inside* the trim, and increase the playhead visual weight so it's clearly distinct from the orange handles.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-04-24 09:26:48Z)**_

Pushed.

**The mouse bug:** `hitTest` had a symmetric 40ms tolerance around each handle — so clicking up to 40ms *past* the end handle was being interpreted as a handle grab, not a playhead move. Now the tolerance zone only extends *inward* from each handle (into the selection). Click anywhere past `endMs` → playhead jumps there; drag it → handle extends. Same on the start side.

**Visual:** the playhead is now a 2px red line with a white outer stroke, so it's clearly distinct from the orange handles even when they overlap.

**On keyboard:** the current code in [ui/main.js:275](ui/main.js#L275) clamps to `[0, durationMs]`, not `[startMs, endMs]`. Arrow-right past `endMs` should move the playhead into the dim region. The readout at the bottom-right of the trim bar shows `head ###` — you can verify it's moving. If you're still seeing arrow-right stop at the end handle, it's very likely a stale browser cache: hard-reload (Cmd+Shift+R) and the new build should kick in. If it still sticks after a hard reload, tell me what `head` shows in the readout when you press `→` at the end and I'll dig further.

---
